bug_description: The loop condition uses `i + size < n` instead of `i + size <= n`. The correct condition includes the window starting at position `i` when `i + size == n` exactly (the window fits perfectly at the end of the list). With `<`, this last valid full window is excluded. The bug only manifests when a window fits exactly at the end — inputs where `(len(items) - start) % stride == 0` and the final window aligns perfectly. It requires understanding the boundary between strict and non-strict comparisons in window iteration.
